mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS over the prior month signals easier policy and a lower discount rate for equities, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if rate cuts are not accompanied by improving broad risk sentiment, because easing during recession scare can be offset by earnings fears.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are negative in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS is falling on a 1-month basis, or if the signal's Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is accelerating, the market prices tighter real financing conditions and lower equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect fails when inflation is high but growth is still reaccelerating and the market treats tightening as credible stabilization rather than constraint.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 6-month median
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the spread between triggered and untriggered average returns is not negative for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent 3-month average signals weakening labor demand and slower future earnings growth, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when unemployment rises from supply-side labor force expansion without a concurrent decline in payroll growth.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are non-negative in more than 55% of instances after a 0.3pp 3-month UNRATE increase, or if no negative return skew appears relative to stable-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: A contemporaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates stronger consumer spending and housing demand, supporting near-term revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal fails when higher sales are driven by price inflation rather than real demand, because equity gains then lag the macro print.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-HOUST expansion does not produce positive 5d SPX returns on average, or if the hit rate is below 55% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO with stable UNRATE identifies an expansion in real output without labor market stress, which should support cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if industrial production gains are purely inventory rebuilds and not accompanied by broad demand improvement.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises over 3 months while UNRATE stays within 0.1 percentage point of its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods do not deliver higher 60d SPX returns than baseline, or if the return differential turns negative for two consecutive rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDPC1 on the latest available quarterly vintage points to broad final-demand weakness, which should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when GDP weakness is offset by aggressive monetary easing that immediately re-rates equities upward.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP contractions are followed by positive 60d SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the signal IC is indistinguishable from zero across any 4-quarter span.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, a higher FEDFUNDS rate combined with flat or rising CPIAUCSL implies persistent real-rate pressure, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect fails when inflation falls fast enough to offset nominal rate pressure and real yields stop rising.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is rising or above 4.0% while CPIAUCSL 1-month inflation is below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered set does not show negative 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the average return under the trigger is not below the unconditional average by at least 20 bps.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in PAYEMS growth and rise in UNRATE signals genuine labor-market deterioration, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down. The mechanism fails if payroll weakness is offset by a simultaneous sharp fall in FEDFUNDS that overwhelms growth concerns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth turns negative and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over the same span
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes fail to generate negative 20d SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean for any 3-year rolling window.
